name: Honey and Lemon Carrageen Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the carrageen in hot water to cover for about 15 minutes (or reconstitute according to package instructions), then drain discarding the soaking liquid.
2. In a separate bowl, mix together the honey, lemon juice, and grated rind with fresh water until well combined.
3. Bring this mixture to a boil and simmer for 25-30 minutes.
4. Strain the mixture and discard the carrageen, allowing it to cool slightly.
5. In another bowl, gently fold the beaten egg white into the whipped cream.
6. Carefully incorporate the egg white and cream mixture into the cooled carrageen liquid.
7. Pour the final pudding mixture into a greased mold and chill until set.
(Carrageen [or "carrageenan"] can usually be found in health food stores.)
